methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $35M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

data.narrative:
Reflect Orbital is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Ben Nowack and Tristan Semmelhack that has raised $35.2M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers (ClearSpace, Hubble Network, Aalyria, and Cowboy Space Corporation) valued between $400M and $2.0B, as well as 5 public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two methods triangulate the valuation. Stage-progression applies a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement to the $35M capital base, yielding $70M. Peer-median valuation across the four private comparables produces $850M, with a range of $400M–$2.0B. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable because the target's revenue is not disclosed. The two applicable methods imply a wide spread reflecting the high variance in private aerospace valuations at this stage.

Triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $60M (floor) to $977M (ceiling), with a median of $460M. The 16x spread between methods reflects genuine uncertainty in early-stage aerospace valuations. The peer-median method weights toward larger comparable rounds; the stage-progression method anchors conservatively to deployed capital. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to the peer set, revenue trajectory visibility, and the specific capital efficiency of Reflect's business model relative to larger-round aerospace ventures.
